denial_letter_references.relevance: Calibrated P2 letter surface: Circular medical-necessity boilerplate.

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

UnitedHealthcare has completed its medical necessity review of the prior authorization request submitted by your provider. Your provider's original request for this therapy was received 47 days before the date of this determination, May 26, 2026.

After review against UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines, we are unable to approve the requested service. Coverage of the requested therapy requires documentation of a defined course of conservative treatment before the requested service is considered medically necessary. The documentation available does not establish the required duration and type of conservative care, and the requested service therefore does not satisfy the applicable criteria in effect.

This determination was processed through our automated utilization management system following resubmission of the request. The request entered review at 9:14 a.m. and the determination was completed at 9:17 a.m. on May 26, 2026. No individualized clinical review of the submitted records was conducted.

If you disagree with this decision, you may request an internal appeal within 180 days of the date of this letter, and you may request an external review by an independent review organization. Appeals may be submitted in writing to UnitedHealthcare Appeals, P.O. Box 740816, Atlanta, GA 30374, or by calling the number on your member ID card.

We understand this decision may be disappointing. This determination addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 32-year-old woman with dermatomyositis (M33.10). She has proximal muscle weakness and a heliotrope rash; her creatine kinase was 3,200 U/L, and EMG plus a muscle biopsy confirmed the diagnosis. Conservative therapy has already failed. She took high-dose prednisone at 1 mg/kg per day, later tapered, and methotrexate at 25 mg weekly for four months over the past eight months, yet her weakness kept progressing and she still cannot rise from a chair or climb stairs unaided. Her rheumatologist then requested intravenous immunoglobulin, which guidelines support for dermatomyositis that remains refractory after corticosteroids and a steroid-sparing agent. The submitted prior authorization included those steroid and methotrexate trials, the CK results, and the biopsy. The original request sat for nearly seven weeks before any decision issued; the denial itself was produced minutes after the file was resubmitted, with no one engaging the records.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches the assigned cell field-for-field; M33.10/IVIG fits infusion_specialty_rx; rationale instantiates conservative_treatment_required.

scenario_realism.reasoning: UHC denying IVIG for refractory dermatomyositis on a conservative-care-not-documented basis is realistic; the 47-day deadline miss and 3-minute automated turnaround mirror documented UHC algorithmic-denial controversies; exploitable gaps.

insurer_voice.reasoning: UnitedHealthcare branding, Medical Management Guidelines, 'documentation does not support' framing, UM sign-off; no cross-insurer tells.

denial_logic.reasoning: Circular conservative-care-not-documented logic with no specified duration/type, admitted no individualized review; not airtight, fits conservative_treatment_required.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Heliotrope rash, CK 3200, EMG/biopsy confirmation, prednisone 1mg/kg + methotrexate 25mg failure, IVIG guideline-supported for refractory DM.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: IVIG is FDA-approved/guideline-supported for refractory dermatomyositis; clean match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: No neighbour shares dermatomyositis or IVIG; only the payer+conservative_treatment_required tag overlaps one osteoporosis/denosumab case, clinical content entirely distinct.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.128 (nearest=case_108_aetna_priorauth), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: No banned topics; autoimmune myopathy; adult 32F; in-scope commercial plan.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender, diagnosis, treatment, dates stable; 47-day original delay vs 3-min resubmission turnaround are distinct events, coherent; conservative-care-not-documented is an appealable falsehood the record rebuts.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Plausible insurer template and human-style clinical summary after de-enumerating the context; no GPT vocabulary, listicles, or templated transitions.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural UHC register with realistic obfuscation; flat delivery of the damning timestamp detail; neutral clinical narrative.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability (standard practice); no dollar figures; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: No invented statutes and no Medicare framing, but appeal window and external-review rights are clearly granted rather than strategically imperfect — generic legal language.
legal_auditor.improvement: Could bury the appeal deadline or omit external review for a more realistic attack vector.

demographic_validator.reasoning: Dermatomyositis has female predominance with adult onset; 32F is epidemiologically apt; consistent female pronouns; no stereotype.

scope_guard.reasoning: UHC in-scope, self-funded employer-sponsored commercial, adult 32, no live-filing/representation language.

date_sanity.reasoning: Determined May 26 2026; original request 47 days prior (~6.7 weeks, consistent with clinical 'nearly seven weeks'); 9:14->9:17 intra-day stamps 3 min apart; 180-day window standard; conservative-therapy timeline nests plausibly.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Generic 'UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines' umbrella reference with no fabricated codes/edition; realistically vague.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Factually false conservative-care premise (8-month steroid+MTX failure documented), 3-minute automated turnaround with no individualized review, and a 47-day deadline miss supporting deemed-denial. Easy appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Factually false premise: denial claims unmet conservative-care duration/type, but records document an 8-month adequate trial of prednisone 1 mg/kg/day plus methotrexate 25 mg weekly that both failed, satisfying the refractory-dermatomyositis IVIG criterion
Procedural/good-faith violation: automated determination completed in 3 minutes (9:14-9:17 a.m.) with explicitly no individualized clinical review of the submitted records
Timeline violation: original request received 47 days before the determination, exceeding the 30-day standard pre-service decision deadline and supporting a deemed-denial argument
strong_defenses: The cited guideline framework itself is legitimate — conditioning IVIG coverage on documented failure of a defined course of conservative treatment is a standard, defensible medical-necessity criterion, so the insurer can argue the policy standard was correct even if its factual application here was wrong
